description:
Create a saltbox-style residential building in SketchUp with the specified dimensions, then use Skelion to install flush-mounted landscape-oriented solar panels covering the long south-facing roof slope.

Specifications:
1. Building Footprint: 36 ft (east-west/red axis) x 26 ft (north-south/green axis). Place the southwest corner at the origin (0, 0, 0).
2. Wall Heights: South wall is 10 ft high. North wall is 20 ft high.
3. Roof: Connect the tops of the walls to form a single long south-facing roof slope (this represents the saltbox roof).
4. Location: Set the model location to Concord, Massachusetts, USA (Latitude: 42.46 N, Longitude: 71.35 W).
5. Solar Panels: Open Skelion and configure panels as 992 mm (width) x 1650 mm (height). Set orientation to Landscape. Set Tilt to 0 degrees (flush-mounted) and row spacing to 0 mm.
6. Placement: Select the south-facing roof surface and use Skelion to insert the solar panels to cover the area.
7. Save the model to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\saltbox_solar.skp'.
